info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: Many individuals of West African descent exhibit a natural genetic resistance to infection by Plasmodium vivax. Which of the following mechanisms best explains this resistance?

options.label: Absence of Duffy blood group antigens on the surface of red blood cells
High concentrations of foetal haemoglobin within mature erythrocytes
Inheritance of the sickle cell trait (haemoglobin S)
Presence of red blood cell Schüffner stippling
Production of PfEMP1 proteins on the erythrocyte membrane

q2.prompt: A 45-year-old hunter presents with high fever, severe myalgias, and periorbital edema two weeks after consuming undercooked bear meat; physical examination also reveals small splinter hemorrhages under his fingernails. Which parasite is the most likely cause?

q3.prompt: A patient with a chronic, latent Strongyloides stercoralis infection is prescribed high-dose corticosteroid therapy for an inflammatory condition. Shortly after, the patient develops a life-threatening hyperinfection syndrome characterized by disseminated larvae and Escherichia coli bacteremia. Which process is primarily responsible for this clinical progression?

options.label: Acceleration of the internal autoinfection cycle
Enhanced maturation of eggs in the external environment
Inhibition of the transition from rhabditiform to filariform larvae
Migration of adult worms from the small bowel into the biliary tract
Promotion of direct skin penetration by rhabditiform larvae

q4.prompt: A biopsy of striated muscle is performed on a patient presenting with high-grade fever, severe myalgia, and periorbital edema after consuming undercooked wild game. The pathologist identifies a characteristic "nurse cell" containing an encysted larva. Which parasite has been identified?

q5.prompt: During the complex life cycle of the Plasmodium species, different morphological stages are observed in the human host. Which of the following stages is specifically responsible for the transmission of the parasite from the human host back to the Anopheles mosquito vector?

q6.prompt: A 45-year-old traveler returning from an endemic region presents with Plasmodium falciparum malaria complicated by an altered level of consciousness and high parasitemia. According to standard clinical guidelines, which of the following is the first-line treatment for this patient?

q7.prompt: A patient diagnosed with Plasmodium vivax malaria is being evaluated for radical curative therapy to prevent future relapses. Before initiating treatment, a quantitative test for the Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase (G6PD) enzyme is required. Which medication is strictly contraindicated if the patient is found to have a severe deficiency of this enzyme?

q8.prompt: A patient presents with migratory subcutaneous swellings and a marked peripheral eosinophilia. The clinical history reveals the frequent consumption of "som fak," a local dish made of raw fermented freshwater fish. Which organism is most likely responsible for this presentation?

q9.prompt: During a physical examination of a child in South America, the physician notes a classic "Romaña sign," characterized by unilateral, painless periorbital swelling. This finding is a classic clinical indicator for which condition?

options.label: Early stage of African sleeping sickness

q10.prompt: Fasciola hepatica and Fasciolopsis buski are both trematodes that can infect humans, but they occupy different primary habitats. Which of the following correctly identifies the adult habitat for each parasite?

options.label: F. hepatica in the bile ducts; F. buski in the small intestine
F. hepatica in the lungs; F. buski in the large intestine
F. hepatica in the mesenteric veins; F. buski in the bile ducts
F. hepatica in the small intestine; F. buski in the bile ducts
F. hepatica in the urinary bladder; F. buski in the small intestine
